hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
Catalog.Sku as "Sku"
  Orders.Ref
  Orders.Loc
  Orders.Day as "OrderDate"
  Orders.RndBaseDemand as "Quantity"
  Orders.RndBaseDemand * Catalog.SellPrice as "NetAmount"
  Catalog.SellPrice
  Orders.WeekBaseDemand
  Catalog.Currency as "Currency"
  Orders.OrderId
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.RndQuantile
  Orders.OrderRndWeight
  Orders.OrderWeightInBucket
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BaseDemand
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BaseDemandPlusGrowth
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.RndBucket
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment
  // Orders.SumRndBaseDemandByBucket
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketBase
  Catalog.Dispersion
  Catalog.Alpha
  order by Orders.Day

///~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
///---------------------------------------EXPORT--------------------------------------------
///~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

show label "Export" a21h21

where Orders.IsPast
  show table "Orders Output" d26h27 write: "\{projectFolder}Input/Orders/Orders_\{itText}.ion" with
    Catalog.Sku as "Sku"
    Orders.Ref
    Orders.Loc
    Orders.Day as "OrderDate"
hits.summary: This snippet creates and exports an orders table, writing selected fields (like SKU, date, quantity, price) to `/Input/Orders/Orders_{itText}.ion`.

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
///Final Rnd Base demand
Orders.RndBaseDemand = if Orders.IsExtremeBimodalDemand or Orders.IsExtremeRightSkewedDemand then Orders.RndAvgBucketDemand * Orders.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor * Orders.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor else Orders.RawRndBaseDemand

show table "Test" a30 with
  Catalog.Sku
  Catalog.IsRightSkewedDemand
  1 /. Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFrequence
  Orders.DayIndex
  Orders.Day
  Orders.IsExtremeRightSkewedDemand
  Orders.RndAvgBucketDemand
  Orders.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor
  Orders.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor
  Orders.RndBaseDemand

Orders.WeekBaseDemand = (UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketBase / Catalog.PeriodGroupSize) * 7

///~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
///--------------------------------------REPORTING------------------------------------------
///~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

///Overall overview

Orders.IsFirstOrderOfWeek = argwhichever() by [Orders.Sku , monday(Orders.Day)]
keep where Orders.RndBaseDemand > 0 or Orders.IsFirstOrderOfWeek

iteration = 1
itText = padLeft(text(iteration),"0",2)
checkDashboard = 51914
hits.summary: This snippet: 1. Adjusts `RndBaseDemand` for extreme demand patterns. 2. Displays a test table with SKU demand metrics. 3. Calculates weekly base demand from bucket data. 4. Filters orders with positive demand or first-of-week.

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
SeasonMonthnum : number

read "/Manual/TopItemsParameters.xlsx" unsafe as TopItemsParameters with
  CategoryDisplayRank : number
  Category : text
  ItemDisplayRank : number
  Ref : text
  Sku : text
  BuyPrice : number
  MarginRatio : number
  BucketSize : number
  BaseDemand : number
  IsSeasonal : boolean
  SeasonalAgressiveness : number
  SeasonMonthnum : number

read "/Manual/Suppliers_Dashboard.ion" as Supplier with
  Supplier : text
  LeadTime : number
  "Order Frequency" as ReorderCycle : number

read form with
  startDate : date
  endDate : date
  testFocusItem : text

///~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
///---------------------------------------------CATALOG---------------------------------------------
///~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

today = date(2023,11,21)/// [Swann 21/11/2023] just created some articles with no sales. So I added a filter on the actionrwd part.
show label "Catalog" a1h1

Catalog.Sku = concat(Catalog.ItemCode,"-",Catalog.ItemLoc)

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor = 2

Orders.IsExtremeBimodalDemand = Catalog.IsBimodalDemand and
                                Orders.DayIndex mod ((1 /. Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFrequence) * Catalog.ReorderCycle / 2) == 0
                                //Division per 2 to fake twice the frequence in the orders generation while we want to display only the frequence in the distribution itself

Orders.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor = if Orders.IsExtremeBimodalDemand then Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor else 1

///Extreme right skewed demand
Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFrequence = if Catalog.IsRightSkewedDemand then 0.1 else 0
Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor = 5

Orders.IsExtremeRightSkewedDemand = Catalog.IsRightSkewedDemand and
                                    Orders.DayIndex mod ((1 /. Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFrequence) * 7) == 0

Orders.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor = if Orders.IsExtremeRightSkewedDemand then
                                              Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor * (0.2 + 0.8 * random.uniform(Orders.0,Orders.1))
    else
                                              1

///Final Rnd Base demand
hits.summary: This snippet configures extreme demand patterns for items, applying factors to orders when specific conditions (like bimodal or right-skewed demand) are met.

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
3  -> Seasons.SteepImpact

Seasons.Impact = if Catalog.IsTopItem then Seasons.Impact ^ 2 else 1.05

Orders.SeasonImpact = same(Seasons.Impact)
                      by [Seasons.WeekNum, Catalog.Sku]
                      at [Orders.Weeknum, Orders.Sku]
UserDefinedTimeOrders.SeasonImpact = avg(Orders.SeasonImpact)

///Bucket base
UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketBase = UserDefinedTimeOrders.BaseDemandPlusGrowth * UserDefinedTimeOrders.SeasonImpact

///Final demand-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
Catalog.Dispersion = mode(Categories.Dispersion) by Categories.SubCategory at Catalog.SubCategory default (1)
Catalog.Alpha = 0
UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment = dirac(0)

where Catalog.StartDate < today
  where UserDefinedTimeOrders.IsPast
    UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment = actionrwd.segment(
      TimeIndex: UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex,
      BaseLine: UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketBase,
      Dispersion: Catalog.Dispersion,
      Alpha: Catalog.Alpha,
      FixedStart : UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex  - 1,
      Start: dirac(0),
      Duration: dirac(1))


  where Catalog.Sku == "DS-141-DC"
hits.summary: Creates seasonal impact multipliers, applies them to orders, and calculates demand segments using base demand and dispersion. Writes to *Orders* and *UserDefinedTimeOrders*.

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
Seasons.IsSeasonal = same(TopItemsParameters.IsSeasonal) by TopItemsParameters.Sku at Catalog.Sku
Seasons.SeasonalAgressiveness = same(TopItemsParameters.SeasonalAgressiveness) by TopItemsParameters.Sku at Catalog.Sku
Seasons.SeasonMonthnum = same(TopItemsParameters.SeasonMonthnum) by TopItemsParameters.Sku at Catalog.Sku

Seasons.SelectedMonthnum = if Seasons.IsSeasonal then Seasons.SeasonMonthnum else
      if Seasons.IsSeasonalSubCategory then Seasons.SubCategorySeasonMonthnum else 1

///Seasons models
pi = 3.14
maxWeekNum = max(Seasons.WeekNum)
maxSeasonalImpact = 2.5
// minSeasonalImpact = 0.5

Seasons.SeasonIndex = ((Seasons.SelectedMonthnum * 4 + 4) - Seasons.WeekNum) * (2 * pi) / maxWeekNum + pi / 2

//Low impact (Bhaskara approximation for sinus function)
sinusParam1 = 16
sinusParam2 = 4
Seasons.ClassicSinus = sinusParam1 * Seasons.SeasonIndex * (pi - Seasons.SeasonIndex) / (5 * pi^2 - sinusParam2 * Seasons.SeasonIndex * (pi - Seasons.SeasonIndex))
Seasons.MinClassicSinus = abs(min(Seasons.ClassicSinus) by Catalog.Sku)
Seasons.PositiveClassicSinus = Seasons.ClassicSinus + Seasons.MinClassicSinus
hits.summary: This snippet assigns seasonal attributes to products (from *TopItemsParameters*) and calculates a seasonal impact curve using a sine approximation. Writes to *Seasons* table.

  Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFrequence

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
sinusParam1 = 3
sinusParam2 = 15
Seasons.SteepSinus = sinusParam1 * Seasons.SeasonIndex * (pi - Seasons.SeasonIndex) / (5 * pi^2 - sinusParam2 * Seasons.SeasonIndex * (pi - Seasons.SeasonIndex))
Seasons.MinSteepSinus = abs(min(Seasons.SteepSinus) by Catalog.Sku)
Seasons.PositiveSteepSinus = Seasons.SteepSinus + Seasons.MinSteepSinus
Seasons.MaxPositiveSteepSinus = max(Seasons.PositiveSteepSinus) by Catalog.Sku

Seasons.SteepImpact = max(Seasons.PositiveSteepSinus, Seasons.MaxPositiveSteepSinus * 0.5) * (maxSeasonalImpact / Seasons.MaxPositiveSteepSinus)

show plot "Seasonal steep impact" d16e16 with
  Seasons.WeekNum
  Seasons.SteepImpact

///Season impact calculation

Seasons.Impact = 1

where Seasons.IsSeasonalSubCategory
  Seasons.Impact = match Seasons.SubCategorySeasonalAgressiveness with //match does not work with negative values !!!
    1  -> Seasons.LowImpact
    2  -> Seasons.MediumImpact
    3  -> Seasons.SteepImpact

where Seasons.IsSeasonal
  Seasons.Impact = match Seasons.SeasonalAgressiveness with //match does not work with negative values !!!
    1  -> Seasons.LowImpact
    2  -> Seasons.MediumImpact
    3  -> Seasons.SteepImpact
hits.summary: Calculates and plots seasonal impact for SKUs using a steep sinusoidal formula, adjusting values by subcategory/seasonal aggressiveness. Writes to `Seasons` table.

hits.text:
Catalog.Sku as "Sku"
  Orders.Ref
  Orders.Loc
  Orders.Day as "OrderDate"
  Orders.RndBaseDemand as "Quantity"
  Orders.RndBaseDemand * Catalog.SellPrice as "NetAmount"
  Catalog.SellPrice
  Orders.WeekBaseDemand
  Catalog.Currency as "Currency"
  Orders.OrderId
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.RndQuantile
  Orders.OrderRndWeight
  Orders.OrderWeightInBucket
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BaseDemand
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BaseDemandPlusGrowth
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.RndBucket
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment
  // Orders.SumRndBaseDemandByBucket
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketBase
  Catalog.Dispersion
  Catalog.Alpha
  order by Orders.Day

///~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
///---------------------------------------EXPORT--------------------------------------------
///~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

show label "Export" a21h21

where Orders.IsPast
  show table "Orders Output" d26h27 write: "\{projectFolder}Input/Orders/Orders_\{itText}.ion" with
    Catalog.Sku as "Sku"
    Orders.Ref
    Orders.Loc
    Orders.Day as "OrderDate"
hits.summary: This snippet creates and exports an orders table, writing selected fields (like SKU, date, quantity, price) to `/Input/Orders/Orders_{itText}.ion`.

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
///Final Rnd Base demand
Orders.RndBaseDemand = if Orders.IsExtremeBimodalDemand or Orders.IsExtremeRightSkewedDemand then Orders.RndAvgBucketDemand * Orders.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor * Orders.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor else Orders.RawRndBaseDemand

show table "Test" a30 with
  Catalog.Sku
  Catalog.IsRightSkewedDemand
  1 /. Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFrequence
  Orders.DayIndex
  Orders.Day
  Orders.IsExtremeRightSkewedDemand
  Orders.RndAvgBucketDemand
  Orders.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor
  Orders.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor
  Orders.RndBaseDemand

Orders.WeekBaseDemand = (UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketBase / Catalog.PeriodGroupSize) * 7

///~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
///--------------------------------------REPORTING------------------------------------------
///~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

///Overall overview

Orders.IsFirstOrderOfWeek = argwhichever() by [Orders.Sku , monday(Orders.Day)]
keep where Orders.RndBaseDemand > 0 or Orders.IsFirstOrderOfWeek

iteration = 1
itText = padLeft(text(iteration),"0",2)
checkDashboard = 51914
hits.summary: This snippet: 1. Adjusts `RndBaseDemand` for extreme demand patterns. 2. Displays a test table with SKU demand metrics. 3. Calculates weekly base demand from bucket data. 4. Filters orders with positive demand or first-of-week.

hits.text:
Catalog.IsBimodalDemand
  Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFrequence

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
Catalog.Sku as "Sku"
  Orders.Ref
  Orders.Loc
  Orders.Day as "OrderDate"
  Orders.RndBaseDemand as "Quantity"
  Orders.RndBaseDemand * Catalog.SellPrice as "NetAmount"
  Catalog.SellPrice
  Orders.WeekBaseDemand
  Catalog.Currency as "Currency"
  Orders.OrderId
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.RndQuantile
  Orders.OrderRndWeight
  Orders.OrderWeightInBucket
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BaseDemand
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BaseDemandPlusGrowth
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.RndBucket
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment
  // Orders.SumRndBaseDemandByBucket
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketBase
  Catalog.Dispersion
  Catalog.Alpha
  order by Orders.Day

///~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
///---------------------------------------EXPORT--------------------------------------------
///~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

show label "Export" a21h21

where Orders.IsPast
  show table "Orders Output" d26h27 write: "\{projectFolder}Input/Orders/Orders_\{itText}.ion" with
    Catalog.Sku as "Sku"
    Orders.Ref
    Orders.Loc
    Orders.Day as "OrderDate"
hits.summary: This snippet creates and exports an orders table, writing selected fields (like SKU, date, quantity, price) to `/Input/Orders/Orders_{itText}.ion`.

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
3  -> Seasons.SteepImpact

Seasons.Impact = if Catalog.IsTopItem then Seasons.Impact ^ 2 else 1.05

Orders.SeasonImpact = same(Seasons.Impact)
                      by [Seasons.WeekNum, Catalog.Sku]
                      at [Orders.Weeknum, Orders.Sku]
UserDefinedTimeOrders.SeasonImpact = avg(Orders.SeasonImpact)

///Bucket base
UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketBase = UserDefinedTimeOrders.BaseDemandPlusGrowth * UserDefinedTimeOrders.SeasonImpact

///Final demand-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
Catalog.Dispersion = mode(Categories.Dispersion) by Categories.SubCategory at Catalog.SubCategory default (1)
Catalog.Alpha = 0
UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment = dirac(0)

where Catalog.StartDate < today
  where UserDefinedTimeOrders.IsPast
    UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment = actionrwd.segment(
      TimeIndex: UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex,
      BaseLine: UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketBase,
      Dispersion: Catalog.Dispersion,
      Alpha: Catalog.Alpha,
      FixedStart : UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex  - 1,
      Start: dirac(0),
      Duration: dirac(1))


  where Catalog.Sku == "DS-141-DC"
hits.summary: Creates seasonal impact multipliers, applies them to orders, and calculates demand segments using base demand and dispersion. Writes to *Orders* and *UserDefinedTimeOrders*.

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment as "DemandSegment"

UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment = truncate(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment,
                                                0,
                                                min(quantile(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment, 0.95), UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketBase * 2))

UserDefinedTimeOrders.ProbaZero = int(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment,0,0)
UserDefinedTimeOrders.MeanDemandSegment = mean(truncate(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment,1,max(1, quantile (UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment, 1))))
where UserDefinedTimeOrders.ProbaZero > 0.15 and UserDefinedTimeOrders.MeanDemandSegment >= 5 //removing bucket 0 to see if it fixes bug ar segment
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment = truncate(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment,1, quantile (UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment, 1))

where Catalog.Sku == "DS-141-DC"
  show scalar "DemandSegment trunc Feb 2, 2023" with same(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment) when(UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex == 264)
  show scalar "DemandSegment trunc Jan 14, 2023" with same(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment) when(UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex == 261)

///Bimodal demand
hits.summary: This snippet adjusts demand segments by capping outliers, calculates zero-demand probability, and refines segments for high-zero-probability items. It logs specific bucket values for a SKU.

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
Orders.RndBucketDemand = quantile(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment, UserDefinedTimeOrders.RndQuantile)
Orders.RndDemand = Orders.RndBucketDemand * Orders.OrderWeightInBucket

///Final daily order demand
Orders.RawRndBaseDemand = max(round(Orders.RndDemand), 0)

Orders.SumRndBaseDemandByBucket = sum(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand) by [Catalog.Sku , Orders.BucketNew]

Orders.IsPickRandomBucket = argfirst() by [Catalog.Sku, Orders.BucketNew] scan Orders.RndDemand
where Orders.IsPickRandomBucket
  Orders.RawRndBaseDemand = max(Orders.RndBucketDemand - Orders.SumRndBaseDemandByBucket, 0)

///Safeguards
Catalog.SalesRanvar = ranvar(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand) by Catalog.Sku at Catalog.Sku
Catalog.SalesRanvarMean = mean(Catalog.SalesRanvar)
Catalog.SalesRanvarStd = sqrt(variance(Catalog.SalesRanvar))

Catalog.SalesRanvarQ99 = quantile(Catalog.SalesRanvar, 0.99)

Orders.RawRndBaseDemand = min(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand, min(Catalog.SalesRanvarMean + 4 * Catalog.SalesRanvarStd, Catalog.SalesRanvarQ99))

///Extreme bimodal demand
Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFrequence = if Catalog.IsBimodalDemand then 0.2 else 0
Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor = 2
hits.summary: Calculates randomized order demand per SKU/bucket, applies safeguards, and adjusts for extreme bimodal demand patterns. Writes to `Orders.RawRndBaseDemand` and `Catalog`.

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.summary: This snippet extracts the top 9 ranked items from **Catalog** and plots their net order amounts (demand × price) from **Orders** in a color-coded line chart.

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment as "DemandSegment"

UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment = truncate(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment,
                                                0,
                                                min(quantile(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment, 0.95), UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketBase * 2))

UserDefinedTimeOrders.ProbaZero = int(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment,0,0)
UserDefinedTimeOrders.MeanDemandSegment = mean(truncate(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment,1,max(1, quantile (UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment, 1))))
where UserDefinedTimeOrders.ProbaZero > 0.15 and UserDefinedTimeOrders.MeanDemandSegment >= 5 //removing bucket 0 to see if it fixes bug ar segment
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment = truncate(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment,1, quantile (UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment, 1))

where Catalog.Sku == "DS-141-DC"
  show scalar "DemandSegment trunc Feb 2, 2023" with same(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment) when(UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex == 264)
  show scalar "DemandSegment trunc Jan 14, 2023" with same(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment) when(UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex == 261)

///Bimodal demand
hits.summary: This snippet adjusts demand segments by capping outliers, calculates zero-demand probability, and refines segments for high-zero-probability items. It logs specific bucket values for a SKU.

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor = 2

Orders.IsExtremeBimodalDemand = Catalog.IsBimodalDemand and
                                Orders.DayIndex mod ((1 /. Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFrequence) * Catalog.ReorderCycle / 2) == 0
                                //Division per 2 to fake twice the frequence in the orders generation while we want to display only the frequence in the distribution itself

Orders.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor = if Orders.IsExtremeBimodalDemand then Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor else 1

///Extreme right skewed demand
Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFrequence = if Catalog.IsRightSkewedDemand then 0.1 else 0
Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor = 5

Orders.IsExtremeRightSkewedDemand = Catalog.IsRightSkewedDemand and
                                    Orders.DayIndex mod ((1 /. Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFrequence) * 7) == 0

Orders.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor = if Orders.IsExtremeRightSkewedDemand then
                                              Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor * (0.2 + 0.8 * random.uniform(Orders.0,Orders.1))
    else
                                              1

///Final Rnd Base demand
hits.summary: This snippet configures extreme demand patterns for items, applying factors to orders when specific conditions (like bimodal or right-skewed demand) are met.

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
show scatter same("\{Catalog.ItemCode} - Bucket Base Demand") by slice a15b16 slices:slice with
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BaseDemand

show scatter same("\{Catalog.ItemCode} - Bucket Base Demand + Growth") by slice a17b18 slices:slice with
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BaseDemandPlusGrowth

show scatter same("\{Catalog.ItemCode} - Bucket Base (Base Demand + Growth + Seasonality)") by slice f14h16 slices:slice with
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketBase

show scatter same("\{Catalog.ItemCode} - Bucket Random quantile") by slice f18h19 slices:slice with
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.RndQuantile

show linechart same("\{Catalog.ItemCode} - Orders generation") by slice c9h12 slices:slice with
  sum(Orders.RndBucketDemand) as "RndBucketDemand"
  sum(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand) as "RawRndBaseDemand"
  sum(Orders.RndBaseDemand) as "RndBaseDemand" {color: #FF7300}
  if any(Orders.IsExtremeBimodalDemand) then 1 else 0 as "Bimodal extreme point" {backgroundColor: "red" ; seriesType: background ; color: #F3E4E4}
hits.summary: Creates scatter and line charts visualizing item demand metrics (base, growth, seasonality, random quantiles) and generated orders from `UserDefinedTimeOrders` and `Orders`.

show scatter same("\{Catalog.ItemCode} - Bucket Base Demand") by slice a15b16 slices:slice with
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BaseDemand

show scatter same("\{Catalog.ItemCode} - Bucket Base Demand + Growth") by slice a17b18 slices:slice with
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BaseDemandPlusGrowth

show scatter same("\{Catalog.ItemCode} - Bucket Base (Base Demand + Growth + Seasonality)") by slice f14h16 slices:slice with
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketBase

show scatter same("\{Catalog.ItemCode} - Bucket Random quantile") by slice f18h19 slices:slice with
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.RndQuantile

show linechart same("\{Catalog.ItemCode} - Orders generation") by slice c9h12 slices:slice with
  sum(Orders.RndBucketDemand) as "RndBucketDemand"
  sum(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand) as "RawRndBaseDemand"
  sum(Orders.RndBaseDemand) as "RndBaseDemand" {color: #FF7300}
  if any(Orders.IsExtremeBimodalDemand) then 1 else 0 as "Bimodal extreme point" {backgroundColor: "red" ; seriesType: background ; color: #F3E4E4}

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
minMarginRatio = min(TopItemsParameters.MarginRatio) when(TopItemsParameters.MarginRatio > 0)
Catalog.DefaultMargin = minMarginRatio * (0.4 +  0.1 * random.uniform(Catalog.0,Catalog.1))
Catalog.MarginRatio = same(TopItemsParameters.MarginRatio)
                      by TopItemsParameters.Sku
                      at Catalog.Sku
                      default Catalog.DefaultMargin

Catalog.SellPrice = Catalog.BuyPrice * (1 + Catalog.MarginRatio)

show table "Catalog test" with
  Catalog.Sku
  Catalog.IsTopItem
  Catalog.BuyPrice
  maxBuyPrice
  Catalog.DefaultBuyPrice
  minMarginRatio
  Catalog.DefaultMargin
  Catalog.MarginRatio

///Base demand--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
defaultBaseDemand = 10
Catalog.CategoryMinBaseDemand = min(TopItemsParameters.BaseDemand)
                                when(TopItemsParameters.BaseDemand > 0)
                                by TopItemsParameters.Category
                                at Catalog.Category
Catalog.RndCategoryMinBaseDemand = (0.5 + 0.3 * random.uniform(Catalog.0,Catalog.1)) * Catalog.CategoryMinBaseDemand
hits.summary: **Summary:** Sets default and SKU-specific margins, calculates sell prices, and defines base demand per category with random variation. Reads *TopItemsParameters*, writes *Catalog*. Displays test table.

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
Seasons.IsSeasonal = same(TopItemsParameters.IsSeasonal) by TopItemsParameters.Sku at Catalog.Sku
Seasons.SeasonalAgressiveness = same(TopItemsParameters.SeasonalAgressiveness) by TopItemsParameters.Sku at Catalog.Sku
Seasons.SeasonMonthnum = same(TopItemsParameters.SeasonMonthnum) by TopItemsParameters.Sku at Catalog.Sku

Seasons.SelectedMonthnum = if Seasons.IsSeasonal then Seasons.SeasonMonthnum else
      if Seasons.IsSeasonalSubCategory then Seasons.SubCategorySeasonMonthnum else 1

///Seasons models
pi = 3.14
maxWeekNum = max(Seasons.WeekNum)
maxSeasonalImpact = 2.5
// minSeasonalImpact = 0.5

Seasons.SeasonIndex = ((Seasons.SelectedMonthnum * 4 + 4) - Seasons.WeekNum) * (2 * pi) / maxWeekNum + pi / 2

//Low impact (Bhaskara approximation for sinus function)
sinusParam1 = 16
sinusParam2 = 4
Seasons.ClassicSinus = sinusParam1 * Seasons.SeasonIndex * (pi - Seasons.SeasonIndex) / (5 * pi^2 - sinusParam2 * Seasons.SeasonIndex * (pi - Seasons.SeasonIndex))
Seasons.MinClassicSinus = abs(min(Seasons.ClassicSinus) by Catalog.Sku)
Seasons.PositiveClassicSinus = Seasons.ClassicSinus + Seasons.MinClassicSinus
hits.summary: This snippet assigns seasonal attributes to products (from *TopItemsParameters*) and calculates a seasonal impact curve using a sine approximation. Writes to *Seasons* table.

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
Orders.RndBucketDemand = quantile(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment, UserDefinedTimeOrders.RndQuantile)
Orders.RndDemand = Orders.RndBucketDemand * Orders.OrderWeightInBucket

///Final daily order demand
Orders.RawRndBaseDemand = max(round(Orders.RndDemand), 0)

Orders.SumRndBaseDemandByBucket = sum(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand) by [Catalog.Sku , Orders.BucketNew]

Orders.IsPickRandomBucket = argfirst() by [Catalog.Sku, Orders.BucketNew] scan Orders.RndDemand
where Orders.IsPickRandomBucket
  Orders.RawRndBaseDemand = max(Orders.RndBucketDemand - Orders.SumRndBaseDemandByBucket, 0)

///Safeguards
Catalog.SalesRanvar = ranvar(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand) by Catalog.Sku at Catalog.Sku
Catalog.SalesRanvarMean = mean(Catalog.SalesRanvar)
Catalog.SalesRanvarStd = sqrt(variance(Catalog.SalesRanvar))

Catalog.SalesRanvarQ99 = quantile(Catalog.SalesRanvar, 0.99)

Orders.RawRndBaseDemand = min(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand, min(Catalog.SalesRanvarMean + 4 * Catalog.SalesRanvarStd, Catalog.SalesRanvarQ99))

///Extreme bimodal demand
Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFrequence = if Catalog.IsBimodalDemand then 0.2 else 0
Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor = 2
hits.summary: Calculates randomized order demand per SKU/bucket, applies safeguards, and adjusts for extreme bimodal demand patterns. Writes to `Orders.RawRndBaseDemand` and `Catalog`.

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor = 2

Orders.IsExtremeBimodalDemand = Catalog.IsBimodalDemand and
                                Orders.DayIndex mod ((1 /. Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFrequence) * Catalog.ReorderCycle / 2) == 0
                                //Division per 2 to fake twice the frequence in the orders generation while we want to display only the frequence in the distribution itself

Orders.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor = if Orders.IsExtremeBimodalDemand then Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor else 1

///Extreme right skewed demand
Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFrequence = if Catalog.IsRightSkewedDemand then 0.1 else 0
Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor = 5

Orders.IsExtremeRightSkewedDemand = Catalog.IsRightSkewedDemand and
                                    Orders.DayIndex mod ((1 /. Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFrequence) * 7) == 0

Orders.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor = if Orders.IsExtremeRightSkewedDemand then
                                              Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor * (0.2 + 0.8 * random.uniform(Orders.0,Orders.1))
    else
                                              1

///Final Rnd Base demand
hits.summary: This snippet configures extreme demand patterns for items, applying factors to orders when specific conditions (like bimodal or right-skewed demand) are met.

Orders.RndBucketDemand = quantile(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment, UserDefinedTimeOrders.RndQuantile)
Orders.RndDemand = Orders.RndBucketDemand * Orders.OrderWeightInBucket

///Final daily order demand

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
3  -> Seasons.SteepImpact

Seasons.Impact = if Catalog.IsTopItem then Seasons.Impact ^ 2 else 1.05

Orders.SeasonImpact = same(Seasons.Impact)
                      by [Seasons.WeekNum, Catalog.Sku]
                      at [Orders.Weeknum, Orders.Sku]
UserDefinedTimeOrders.SeasonImpact = avg(Orders.SeasonImpact)

///Bucket base
UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketBase = UserDefinedTimeOrders.BaseDemandPlusGrowth * UserDefinedTimeOrders.SeasonImpact

///Final demand-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
Catalog.Dispersion = mode(Categories.Dispersion) by Categories.SubCategory at Catalog.SubCategory default (1)
Catalog.Alpha = 0
UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment = dirac(0)

where Catalog.StartDate < today
  where UserDefinedTimeOrders.IsPast
    UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment = actionrwd.segment(
      TimeIndex: UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex,
      BaseLine: UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketBase,
      Dispersion: Catalog.Dispersion,
      Alpha: Catalog.Alpha,
      FixedStart : UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex  - 1,
      Start: dirac(0),
      Duration: dirac(1))


  where Catalog.Sku == "DS-141-DC"
hits.summary: Creates seasonal impact multipliers, applies them to orders, and calculates demand segments using base demand and dispersion. Writes to *Orders* and *UserDefinedTimeOrders*.

Orders.RndBaseDemand = if Orders.IsExtremeBimodalDemand or Orders.IsExtremeRightSkewedDemand then Orders.RndAvgBucketDemand * Orders.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor * Orders.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor else Orders.RawRndBaseDemand

show table "Test" a30 with

  Catalog.IsRightSkewedDemand
  1 /. Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFrequence

Orders.WeekBaseDemand = (UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketBase / Catalog.PeriodGroupSize) * 7

///~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
///--------------------------------------REPORTING------------------------------------------
///~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

///Overall overview

Orders.IsFirstOrderOfWeek = argwhichever() by [Orders.Sku , monday(Orders.Day)]
keep where Orders.RndBaseDemand > 0 or Orders.IsFirstOrderOfWeek

iteration = 1
itText = padLeft(text(iteration),"0",2)
checkDashboard = 51914

hits.text:
formendDate = if endDate == date(2001,01,01) then today() else endDate

keep where Orders.Day >= formstartDate and Orders.Day <= (formendDate + 365)

Orders.OrderId = concat("O-",padLeft(text(Orders.Day - date(2001,01,01)),"0",6))

Orders.BucketNew = ceiling(Orders.DayIndex / Catalog.PeriodGroupSize)

Orders.IsPast = Orders.Day <= formendDate
UserDefinedTimeOrders.IsPast = any(Orders.IsPast)

///Seasonality--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

///Weekly catalog
Catalog.CountWeeks = 54
table Seasons = extend.range(Catalog.CountWeeks)
Seasons.WeekNum = Seasons.N

Seasons.SubCategory = Catalog.SubCategory

///Seasons parameters
Seasons.IsSeasonalSubCategory = same(TopSubCategories.IsSeasonal) by TopSubCategories.SubCategory at Catalog.SubCategory
Seasons.SubCategorySeasonalAgressiveness = same(TopSubCategories.SeasonalAgressiveness) by TopSubCategories.SubCategory at Catalog.SubCategory
Seasons.SubCategorySeasonMonthnum = same(TopSubCategories.SeasonMonthnum) by TopSubCategories.SubCategory at Catalog.SubCategory

Seasons.IsSeasonal = same(TopItemsParameters.IsSeasonal) by TopItemsParameters.Sku at Catalog.Sku

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment as "DemandSegment"

UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment = truncate(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment,
                                                0,
                                                min(quantile(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment, 0.95), UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketBase * 2))

UserDefinedTimeOrders.ProbaZero = int(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment,0,0)
UserDefinedTimeOrders.MeanDemandSegment = mean(truncate(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment,1,max(1, quantile (UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment, 1))))
where UserDefinedTimeOrders.ProbaZero > 0.15 and UserDefinedTimeOrders.MeanDemandSegment >= 5 //removing bucket 0 to see if it fixes bug ar segment
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment = truncate(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment,1, quantile (UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment, 1))

where Catalog.Sku == "DS-141-DC"
  show scalar "DemandSegment trunc Feb 2, 2023" with same(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment) when(UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex == 264)

hits.text:
Seasons.IsSeasonal = same(TopItemsParameters.IsSeasonal) by TopItemsParameters.Sku at Catalog.Sku
Seasons.SeasonalAgressiveness = same(TopItemsParameters.SeasonalAgressiveness) by TopItemsParameters.Sku at Catalog.Sku
Seasons.SeasonMonthnum = same(TopItemsParameters.SeasonMonthnum) by TopItemsParameters.Sku at Catalog.Sku

Seasons.SelectedMonthnum = if Seasons.IsSeasonal then Seasons.SeasonMonthnum else
      if Seasons.IsSeasonalSubCategory then Seasons.SubCategorySeasonMonthnum else 1

///Seasons models
pi = 3.14
maxWeekNum = max(Seasons.WeekNum)
maxSeasonalImpact = 2.5
// minSeasonalImpact = 0.5

Seasons.SeasonIndex = ((Seasons.SelectedMonthnum * 4 + 4) - Seasons.WeekNum) * (2 * pi) / maxWeekNum + pi / 2

//Low impact (Bhaskara approximation for sinus function)
sinusParam1 = 16
sinusParam2 = 4
Seasons.ClassicSinus = sinusParam1 * Seasons.SeasonIndex * (pi - Seasons.SeasonIndex) / (5 * pi^2 - sinusParam2 * Seasons.SeasonIndex * (pi - Seasons.SeasonIndex))
Seasons.MinClassicSinus = abs(min(Seasons.ClassicSinus) by Catalog.Sku)
Seasons.PositiveClassicSinus = Seasons.ClassicSinus + Seasons.MinClassicSinus
hits.summary: This snippet assigns seasonal attributes to products (from *TopItemsParameters*) and calculates a seasonal impact curve using a sine approximation. Writes to *Seasons* table.

sinusParam1 = 3
sinusParam2 = 15
Seasons.SteepSinus = sinusParam1 * Seasons.SeasonIndex * (pi - Seasons.SeasonIndex) / (5 * pi^2 - sinusParam2 * Seasons.SeasonIndex * (pi - Seasons.SeasonIndex))
Seasons.MinSteepSinus = abs(min(Seasons.SteepSinus) by Catalog.Sku)
Seasons.PositiveSteepSinus = Seasons.SteepSinus + Seasons.MinSteepSinus
Seasons.MaxPositiveSteepSinus = max(Seasons.PositiveSteepSinus) by Catalog.Sku

Seasons.SteepImpact = max(Seasons.PositiveSteepSinus, Seasons.MaxPositiveSteepSinus * 0.5) * (maxSeasonalImpact / Seasons.MaxPositiveSteepSinus)

show plot "Seasonal steep impact" d16e16 with
  Seasons.WeekNum
  Seasons.SteepImpact

///Season impact calculation

Seasons.Impact = 1

where Seasons.IsSeasonalSubCategory
  Seasons.Impact = match Seasons.SubCategorySeasonalAgressiveness with //match does not work with negative values !!!
    1  -> Seasons.LowImpact
    2  -> Seasons.MediumImpact
    3  -> Seasons.SteepImpact

where Seasons.IsSeasonal
  Seasons.Impact = match Seasons.SeasonalAgressiveness with //match does not work with negative values !!!
    1  -> Seasons.LowImpact
    2  -> Seasons.MediumImpact
    3  -> Seasons.SteepImpact

hits.text:
Seasons.IsSeasonal = same(TopItemsParameters.IsSeasonal) by TopItemsParameters.Sku at Catalog.Sku
Seasons.SeasonalAgressiveness = same(TopItemsParameters.SeasonalAgressiveness) by TopItemsParameters.Sku at Catalog.Sku
Seasons.SeasonMonthnum = same(TopItemsParameters.SeasonMonthnum) by TopItemsParameters.Sku at Catalog.Sku

Seasons.SelectedMonthnum = if Seasons.IsSeasonal then Seasons.SeasonMonthnum else
      if Seasons.IsSeasonalSubCategory then Seasons.SubCategorySeasonMonthnum else 1

///Seasons models
pi = 3.14
maxWeekNum = max(Seasons.WeekNum)
maxSeasonalImpact = 2.5
// minSeasonalImpact = 0.5

Seasons.SeasonIndex = ((Seasons.SelectedMonthnum * 4 + 4) - Seasons.WeekNum) * (2 * pi) / maxWeekNum + pi / 2

//Low impact (Bhaskara approximation for sinus function)
sinusParam1 = 16
sinusParam2 = 4
Seasons.ClassicSinus = sinusParam1 * Seasons.SeasonIndex * (pi - Seasons.SeasonIndex) / (5 * pi^2 - sinusParam2 * Seasons.SeasonIndex * (pi - Seasons.SeasonIndex))
Seasons.MinClassicSinus = abs(min(Seasons.ClassicSinus) by Catalog.Sku)
Seasons.PositiveClassicSinus = Seasons.ClassicSinus + Seasons.MinClassicSinus
hits.summary: This snippet assigns seasonal attributes to products (from *TopItemsParameters*) and calculates a seasonal impact curve using a sine approximation. Writes to *Seasons* table.

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
sinusParam1 = 3
sinusParam2 = 15
Seasons.SteepSinus = sinusParam1 * Seasons.SeasonIndex * (pi - Seasons.SeasonIndex) / (5 * pi^2 - sinusParam2 * Seasons.SeasonIndex * (pi - Seasons.SeasonIndex))
Seasons.MinSteepSinus = abs(min(Seasons.SteepSinus) by Catalog.Sku)
Seasons.PositiveSteepSinus = Seasons.SteepSinus + Seasons.MinSteepSinus
Seasons.MaxPositiveSteepSinus = max(Seasons.PositiveSteepSinus) by Catalog.Sku

Seasons.SteepImpact = max(Seasons.PositiveSteepSinus, Seasons.MaxPositiveSteepSinus * 0.5) * (maxSeasonalImpact / Seasons.MaxPositiveSteepSinus)

show plot "Seasonal steep impact" d16e16 with
  Seasons.WeekNum
  Seasons.SteepImpact

///Season impact calculation

Seasons.Impact = 1

where Seasons.IsSeasonalSubCategory
  Seasons.Impact = match Seasons.SubCategorySeasonalAgressiveness with //match does not work with negative values !!!
    1  -> Seasons.LowImpact
    2  -> Seasons.MediumImpact
    3  -> Seasons.SteepImpact

where Seasons.IsSeasonal
  Seasons.Impact = match Seasons.SeasonalAgressiveness with //match does not work with negative values !!!
    1  -> Seasons.LowImpact
    2  -> Seasons.MediumImpact
    3  -> Seasons.SteepImpact
hits.summary: Calculates and plots seasonal impact for SKUs using a steep sinusoidal formula, adjusting values by subcategory/seasonal aggressiveness. Writes to `Seasons` table.

hits.text:
Orders.RndBucketDemand = quantile(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment, UserDefinedTimeOrders.RndQuantile)
Orders.RndDemand = Orders.RndBucketDemand * Orders.OrderWeightInBucket

///Final daily order demand
Orders.RawRndBaseDemand = max(round(Orders.RndDemand), 0)

Orders.SumRndBaseDemandByBucket = sum(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand) by [Catalog.Sku , Orders.BucketNew]

Orders.IsPickRandomBucket = argfirst() by [Catalog.Sku, Orders.BucketNew] scan Orders.RndDemand
where Orders.IsPickRandomBucket
  Orders.RawRndBaseDemand = max(Orders.RndBucketDemand - Orders.SumRndBaseDemandByBucket, 0)

///Safeguards
Catalog.SalesRanvar = ranvar(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand) by Catalog.Sku at Catalog.Sku
Catalog.SalesRanvarMean = mean(Catalog.SalesRanvar)
Catalog.SalesRanvarStd = sqrt(variance(Catalog.SalesRanvar))

Catalog.SalesRanvarQ99 = quantile(Catalog.SalesRanvar, 0.99)

Orders.RawRndBaseDemand = min(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand, min(Catalog.SalesRanvarMean + 4 * Catalog.SalesRanvarStd, Catalog.SalesRanvarQ99))

///Extreme bimodal demand
Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFrequence = if Catalog.IsBimodalDemand then 0.2 else 0
Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor = 2

hits.text:
Seasons.IsSeasonal = same(TopItemsParameters.IsSeasonal) by TopItemsParameters.Sku at Catalog.Sku
Seasons.SeasonalAgressiveness = same(TopItemsParameters.SeasonalAgressiveness) by TopItemsParameters.Sku at Catalog.Sku
Seasons.SeasonMonthnum = same(TopItemsParameters.SeasonMonthnum) by TopItemsParameters.Sku at Catalog.Sku

Seasons.SelectedMonthnum = if Seasons.IsSeasonal then Seasons.SeasonMonthnum else
      if Seasons.IsSeasonalSubCategory then Seasons.SubCategorySeasonMonthnum else 1

///Seasons models
pi = 3.14
maxWeekNum = max(Seasons.WeekNum)
maxSeasonalImpact = 2.5
// minSeasonalImpact = 0.5

Seasons.SeasonIndex = ((Seasons.SelectedMonthnum * 4 + 4) - Seasons.WeekNum) * (2 * pi) / maxWeekNum + pi / 2

//Low impact (Bhaskara approximation for sinus function)
sinusParam1 = 16
sinusParam2 = 4
Seasons.ClassicSinus = sinusParam1 * Seasons.SeasonIndex * (pi - Seasons.SeasonIndex) / (5 * pi^2 - sinusParam2 * Seasons.SeasonIndex * (pi - Seasons.SeasonIndex))
Seasons.MinClassicSinus = abs(min(Seasons.ClassicSinus) by Catalog.Sku)
Seasons.PositiveClassicSinus = Seasons.ClassicSinus + Seasons.MinClassicSinus
hits.summary: This snippet assigns seasonal attributes to products (from *TopItemsParameters*) and calculates a seasonal impact curve using a sine approximation. Writes to *Seasons* table.

hits.text:
sinusParam1 = 3
sinusParam2 = 15
Seasons.SteepSinus = sinusParam1 * Seasons.SeasonIndex * (pi - Seasons.SeasonIndex) / (5 * pi^2 - sinusParam2 * Seasons.SeasonIndex * (pi - Seasons.SeasonIndex))
Seasons.MinSteepSinus = abs(min(Seasons.SteepSinus) by Catalog.Sku)
Seasons.PositiveSteepSinus = Seasons.SteepSinus + Seasons.MinSteepSinus
Seasons.MaxPositiveSteepSinus = max(Seasons.PositiveSteepSinus) by Catalog.Sku

Seasons.SteepImpact = max(Seasons.PositiveSteepSinus, Seasons.MaxPositiveSteepSinus * 0.5) * (maxSeasonalImpact / Seasons.MaxPositiveSteepSinus)

show plot "Seasonal steep impact" d16e16 with
  Seasons.WeekNum
  Seasons.SteepImpact

///Season impact calculation

Seasons.Impact = 1

where Seasons.IsSeasonalSubCategory
  Seasons.Impact = match Seasons.SubCategorySeasonalAgressiveness with //match does not work with negative values !!!
    1  -> Seasons.LowImpact
    2  -> Seasons.MediumImpact
    3  -> Seasons.SteepImpact

where Seasons.IsSeasonal
  Seasons.Impact = match Seasons.SeasonalAgressiveness with //match does not work with negative values !!!
    1  -> Seasons.LowImpact
    2  -> Seasons.MediumImpact
    3  -> Seasons.SteepImpact
hits.summary: Calculates and plots seasonal impact for SKUs using a steep sinusoidal formula, adjusting values by subcategory/seasonal aggressiveness. Writes to `Seasons` table.

formendDate = if endDate == date(2001,01,01) then today() else endDate

keep where Orders.Day >= formstartDate and Orders.Day <= (formendDate + 365)

Orders.OrderId = concat("O-",padLeft(text(Orders.Day - date(2001,01,01)),"0",6))

Orders.BucketNew = ceiling(Orders.DayIndex / Catalog.PeriodGroupSize)

Orders.IsPast = Orders.Day <= formendDate
UserDefinedTimeOrders.IsPast = any(Orders.IsPast)

///Seasonality--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

///Weekly catalog
Catalog.CountWeeks = 54
table Seasons = extend.range(Catalog.CountWeeks)
Seasons.WeekNum = Seasons.N

Seasons.SubCategory = Catalog.SubCategory

///Seasons parameters
Seasons.IsSeasonalSubCategory = same(TopSubCategories.IsSeasonal) by TopSubCategories.SubCategory at Catalog.SubCategory
Seasons.SubCategorySeasonalAgressiveness = same(TopSubCategories.SeasonalAgressiveness) by TopSubCategories.SubCategory at Catalog.SubCategory
Seasons.SubCategorySeasonMonthnum = same(TopSubCategories.SeasonMonthnum) by TopSubCategories.SubCategory at Catalog.SubCategory

Seasons.IsSeasonal = same(TopItemsParameters.IsSeasonal) by TopItemsParameters.Sku at Catalog.Sku

Catalog.Dispersion = mode(Categories.Dispersion) by Categories.SubCategory at Catalog.SubCategory default (1)

hits.text:
Orders.RndBucketDemand = quantile(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment, UserDefinedTimeOrders.RndQuantile)
Orders.RndDemand = Orders.RndBucketDemand * Orders.OrderWeightInBucket

///Final daily order demand
Orders.RawRndBaseDemand = max(round(Orders.RndDemand), 0)

Orders.SumRndBaseDemandByBucket = sum(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand) by [Catalog.Sku , Orders.BucketNew]

Orders.IsPickRandomBucket = argfirst() by [Catalog.Sku, Orders.BucketNew] scan Orders.RndDemand
where Orders.IsPickRandomBucket
  Orders.RawRndBaseDemand = max(Orders.RndBucketDemand - Orders.SumRndBaseDemandByBucket, 0)

///Safeguards
Catalog.SalesRanvar = ranvar(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand) by Catalog.Sku at Catalog.Sku
Catalog.SalesRanvarMean = mean(Catalog.SalesRanvar)
Catalog.SalesRanvarStd = sqrt(variance(Catalog.SalesRanvar))

Catalog.SalesRanvarQ99 = quantile(Catalog.SalesRanvar, 0.99)

Orders.RawRndBaseDemand = min(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand, min(Catalog.SalesRanvarMean + 4 * Catalog.SalesRanvarStd, Catalog.SalesRanvarQ99))

///Extreme bimodal demand
Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFrequence = if Catalog.IsBimodalDemand then 0.2 else 0
Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor = 2
hits.summary: Calculates randomized order demand per SKU/bucket, applies safeguards, and adjusts for extreme bimodal demand patterns. Writes to `Orders.RawRndBaseDemand` and `Catalog`.

hits.text:
Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor = 2

Orders.IsExtremeBimodalDemand = Catalog.IsBimodalDemand and
                                Orders.DayIndex mod ((1 /. Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFrequence) * Catalog.ReorderCycle / 2) == 0
                                //Division per 2 to fake twice the frequence in the orders generation while we want to display only the frequence in the distribution itself

Orders.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor = if Orders.IsExtremeBimodalDemand then Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor else 1

///Extreme right skewed demand
Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFrequence = if Catalog.IsRightSkewedDemand then 0.1 else 0
Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor = 5

Orders.IsExtremeRightSkewedDemand = Catalog.IsRightSkewedDemand and
                                    Orders.DayIndex mod ((1 /. Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFrequence) * 7) == 0

Orders.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor = if Orders.IsExtremeRightSkewedDemand then
                                              Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor * (0.2 + 0.8 * random.uniform(Orders.0,Orders.1))
    else
                                              1

///Final Rnd Base demand

Catalog.AnnualGrowth = if Catalog.IsTopItem then 0.05 else 0.025
Catalog.BucketGrowth = Catalog.AnnualGrowth * (Catalog.BucketSize / 365)

UserDefinedTimeOrders.BaseDemandPlusGrowth = UserDefinedTimeOrders.BaseDemand * (1 + Catalog.BucketGrowth * UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex)

///Orders creation----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

///Table creation
UserDefinedTimeOrders.DayCount = Catalog.PeriodGroupSize
table Orders = extend.range(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DayCount)

Orders.SubCategory = Catalog.SubCategory
Orders.Ref = Catalog.ItemCode
Orders.Sku = Catalog.Sku
Orders.Loc = Catalog.ItemLoc

Orders.AllDays = count(Orders.*) by Orders.Sku
Orders.DayIndex = Orders.AllDays - rankrev () by Catalog.Sku scan -Orders.1
Orders.Day = Catalog.StartDate + Orders.DayIndex
Orders.Weeknum = weekNum(Orders.Day)

///Scope
show form "Define Start and End" a5b7        {formButton: "[OK]"} with
  startDate as "Start Date (Incl.)"
  endDate as "End Date (Incl.)"
formstartDate = if startDate == date(2001,01,01) then today() - 365 * 15 else startDate
formendDate = if endDate == date(2001,01,01) then today() else endDate

hits.text:
UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment as "DemandSegment"

UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment = truncate(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment,
                                                0,
                                                min(quantile(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment, 0.95), UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketBase * 2))

UserDefinedTimeOrders.ProbaZero = int(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment,0,0)
UserDefinedTimeOrders.MeanDemandSegment = mean(truncate(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment,1,max(1, quantile (UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment, 1))))
where UserDefinedTimeOrders.ProbaZero > 0.15 and UserDefinedTimeOrders.MeanDemandSegment >= 5 //removing bucket 0 to see if it fixes bug ar segment
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment = truncate(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment,1, quantile (UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment, 1))

where Catalog.Sku == "DS-141-DC"
  show scalar "DemandSegment trunc Feb 2, 2023" with same(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment) when(UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex == 264)
  show scalar "DemandSegment trunc Jan 14, 2023" with same(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment) when(UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex == 261)

///Bimodal demand
hits.summary: This snippet adjusts demand segments by capping outliers, calculates zero-demand probability, and refines segments for high-zero-probability items. It logs specific bucket values for a SKU.

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor = 2

Orders.IsExtremeBimodalDemand = Catalog.IsBimodalDemand and
                                Orders.DayIndex mod ((1 /. Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFrequence) * Catalog.ReorderCycle / 2) == 0
                                //Division per 2 to fake twice the frequence in the orders generation while we want to display only the frequence in the distribution itself

Orders.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor = if Orders.IsExtremeBimodalDemand then Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor else 1

///Extreme right skewed demand
Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFrequence = if Catalog.IsRightSkewedDemand then 0.1 else 0
Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor = 5

Orders.IsExtremeRightSkewedDemand = Catalog.IsRightSkewedDemand and
                                    Orders.DayIndex mod ((1 /. Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFrequence) * 7) == 0

Orders.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor = if Orders.IsExtremeRightSkewedDemand then
                                              Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor * (0.2 + 0.8 * random.uniform(Orders.0,Orders.1))
    else
                                              1

///Final Rnd Base demand
hits.summary: This snippet configures extreme demand patterns for items, applying factors to orders when specific conditions (like bimodal or right-skewed demand) are met.

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
Orders.RndBucketDemand = quantile(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment, UserDefinedTimeOrders.RndQuantile)
Orders.RndDemand = Orders.RndBucketDemand * Orders.OrderWeightInBucket

///Final daily order demand
Orders.RawRndBaseDemand = max(round(Orders.RndDemand), 0)

Orders.SumRndBaseDemandByBucket = sum(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand) by [Catalog.Sku , Orders.BucketNew]

Orders.IsPickRandomBucket = argfirst() by [Catalog.Sku, Orders.BucketNew] scan Orders.RndDemand
where Orders.IsPickRandomBucket
  Orders.RawRndBaseDemand = max(Orders.RndBucketDemand - Orders.SumRndBaseDemandByBucket, 0)

///Safeguards
Catalog.SalesRanvar = ranvar(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand) by Catalog.Sku at Catalog.Sku
Catalog.SalesRanvarMean = mean(Catalog.SalesRanvar)
Catalog.SalesRanvarStd = sqrt(variance(Catalog.SalesRanvar))

Catalog.SalesRanvarQ99 = quantile(Catalog.SalesRanvar, 0.99)

Orders.RawRndBaseDemand = min(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand, min(Catalog.SalesRanvarMean + 4 * Catalog.SalesRanvarStd, Catalog.SalesRanvarQ99))

///Extreme bimodal demand
Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFrequence = if Catalog.IsBimodalDemand then 0.2 else 0
Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor = 2
hits.summary: Calculates randomized order demand per SKU/bucket, applies safeguards, and adjusts for extreme bimodal demand patterns. Writes to `Orders.RawRndBaseDemand` and `Catalog`.

hits.text:
3  -> Seasons.SteepImpact

Seasons.Impact = if Catalog.IsTopItem then Seasons.Impact ^ 2 else 1.05

Orders.SeasonImpact = same(Seasons.Impact)
                      by [Seasons.WeekNum, Catalog.Sku]
                      at [Orders.Weeknum, Orders.Sku]
UserDefinedTimeOrders.SeasonImpact = avg(Orders.SeasonImpact)

///Bucket base
UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketBase = UserDefinedTimeOrders.BaseDemandPlusGrowth * UserDefinedTimeOrders.SeasonImpact

///Final demand-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
Catalog.Dispersion = mode(Categories.Dispersion) by Categories.SubCategory at Catalog.SubCategory default (1)
Catalog.Alpha = 0
UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment = dirac(0)

where Catalog.StartDate < today
  where UserDefinedTimeOrders.IsPast
    UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment = actionrwd.segment(
      TimeIndex: UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex,
      BaseLine: UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketBase,
      Dispersion: Catalog.Dispersion,
      Alpha: Catalog.Alpha,
      FixedStart : UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex  - 1,
      Start: dirac(0),
      Duration: dirac(1))


  where Catalog.Sku == "DS-141-DC"
hits.summary: Creates seasonal impact multipliers, applies them to orders, and calculates demand segments using base demand and dispersion. Writes to *Orders* and *UserDefinedTimeOrders*.

                      default Catalog.DefaultMargin

defaultBaseDemand = 10

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.summary: This snippet extracts the top 9 ranked items from **Catalog** and plots their net order amounts (demand × price) from **Orders** in a color-coded line chart.

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
Orders.RndBucketDemand = quantile(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment, UserDefinedTimeOrders.RndQuantile)
Orders.RndDemand = Orders.RndBucketDemand * Orders.OrderWeightInBucket

///Final daily order demand
Orders.RawRndBaseDemand = max(round(Orders.RndDemand), 0)

Orders.SumRndBaseDemandByBucket = sum(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand) by [Catalog.Sku , Orders.BucketNew]

Orders.IsPickRandomBucket = argfirst() by [Catalog.Sku, Orders.BucketNew] scan Orders.RndDemand
where Orders.IsPickRandomBucket
  Orders.RawRndBaseDemand = max(Orders.RndBucketDemand - Orders.SumRndBaseDemandByBucket, 0)

///Safeguards
Catalog.SalesRanvar = ranvar(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand) by Catalog.Sku at Catalog.Sku
Catalog.SalesRanvarMean = mean(Catalog.SalesRanvar)
Catalog.SalesRanvarStd = sqrt(variance(Catalog.SalesRanvar))

Catalog.SalesRanvarQ99 = quantile(Catalog.SalesRanvar, 0.99)

Orders.RawRndBaseDemand = min(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand, min(Catalog.SalesRanvarMean + 4 * Catalog.SalesRanvarStd, Catalog.SalesRanvarQ99))

///Extreme bimodal demand
Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFrequence = if Catalog.IsBimodalDemand then 0.2 else 0
Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor = 2
hits.summary: Calculates randomized order demand per SKU/bucket, applies safeguards, and adjusts for extreme bimodal demand patterns. Writes to `Orders.RawRndBaseDemand` and `Catalog`.

hits.summary: Calculates weekly sales revenue by item rank (4-9) from **Orders** and **Catalog**, then charts order quantities for ranks 1-4.

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation

show linechart same("\{Catalog.ItemCode} - Orders generation") by slice c9h12 slices:slice with
  sum(Orders.RndBucketDemand) as "RndBucketDemand"
  sum(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand) as "RawRndBaseDemand"
  sum(Orders.RndBaseDemand) as "RndBaseDemand" {color: #FF7300}

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
Orders.RndBucketDemand = quantile(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment, UserDefinedTimeOrders.RndQuantile)
Orders.RndDemand = Orders.RndBucketDemand * Orders.OrderWeightInBucket

///Final daily order demand
Orders.RawRndBaseDemand = max(round(Orders.RndDemand), 0)

Orders.SumRndBaseDemandByBucket = sum(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand) by [Catalog.Sku , Orders.BucketNew]

Orders.IsPickRandomBucket = argfirst() by [Catalog.Sku, Orders.BucketNew] scan Orders.RndDemand
where Orders.IsPickRandomBucket
  Orders.RawRndBaseDemand = max(Orders.RndBucketDemand - Orders.SumRndBaseDemandByBucket, 0)

///Safeguards
Catalog.SalesRanvar = ranvar(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand) by Catalog.Sku at Catalog.Sku
Catalog.SalesRanvarMean = mean(Catalog.SalesRanvar)
Catalog.SalesRanvarStd = sqrt(variance(Catalog.SalesRanvar))

Catalog.SalesRanvarQ99 = quantile(Catalog.SalesRanvar, 0.99)

Orders.RawRndBaseDemand = min(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand, min(Catalog.SalesRanvarMean + 4 * Catalog.SalesRanvarStd, Catalog.SalesRanvarQ99))

///Extreme bimodal demand
Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFrequence = if Catalog.IsBimodalDemand then 0.2 else 0
Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor = 2
hits.summary: Calculates randomized order demand per SKU/bucket, applies safeguards, and adjusts for extreme bimodal demand patterns. Writes to `Orders.RawRndBaseDemand` and `Catalog`.

Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor = 2

Orders.IsExtremeBimodalDemand = Catalog.IsBimodalDemand and
                                Orders.DayIndex mod ((1 /. Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFrequence) * Catalog.ReorderCycle / 2) == 0
                                //Division per 2 to fake twice the frequence in the orders generation while we want to display only the frequence in the distribution itself

Orders.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor = if Orders.IsExtremeBimodalDemand then Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor else 1

///Extreme right skewed demand
Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFrequence = if Catalog.IsRightSkewedDemand then 0.1 else 0
Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor = 5

Orders.IsExtremeRightSkewedDemand = Catalog.IsRightSkewedDemand and
                                    Orders.DayIndex mod ((1 /. Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFrequence) * 7) == 0

Orders.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor = if Orders.IsExtremeRightSkewedDemand then
                                              Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor * (0.2 + 0.8 * random.uniform(Orders.0,Orders.1))
    else
                                              1

///Final Rnd Base demand

hits.text:
Catalog.Sku as "Sku"
  Orders.Ref
  Orders.Loc
  Orders.Day as "OrderDate"
  Orders.RndBaseDemand as "Quantity"
  Orders.RndBaseDemand * Catalog.SellPrice as "NetAmount"
  Catalog.SellPrice
  Orders.WeekBaseDemand
  Catalog.Currency as "Currency"
  Orders.OrderId
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.RndQuantile
  Orders.OrderRndWeight
  Orders.OrderWeightInBucket
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BaseDemand
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BaseDemandPlusGrowth
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.RndBucket
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment
  // Orders.SumRndBaseDemandByBucket
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketBase
  Catalog.Dispersion
  Catalog.Alpha
  order by Orders.Day

///~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
///---------------------------------------EXPORT--------------------------------------------
///~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

show label "Export" a21h21

where Orders.IsPast
  show table "Orders Output" d26h27 write: "\{projectFolder}Input/Orders/Orders_\{itText}.ion" with
    Catalog.Sku as "Sku"
    Orders.Ref
    Orders.Loc
    Orders.Day as "OrderDate"
hits.summary: This snippet creates and exports an orders table, writing selected fields (like SKU, date, quantity, price) to `/Input/Orders/Orders_{itText}.ion`.

Catalog.IsBimodalDemand
  Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFrequence
  Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor
  Catalog.IsRightSkewedDemand

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
///Final Rnd Base demand
Orders.RndBaseDemand = if Orders.IsExtremeBimodalDemand or Orders.IsExtremeRightSkewedDemand then Orders.RndAvgBucketDemand * Orders.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor * Orders.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor else Orders.RawRndBaseDemand

show table "Test" a30 with
  Catalog.Sku
  Catalog.IsRightSkewedDemand
  1 /. Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFrequence
  Orders.DayIndex
  Orders.Day
  Orders.IsExtremeRightSkewedDemand
  Orders.RndAvgBucketDemand
  Orders.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor
  Orders.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor
  Orders.RndBaseDemand

Orders.WeekBaseDemand = (UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketBase / Catalog.PeriodGroupSize) * 7

///~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
///--------------------------------------REPORTING------------------------------------------
///~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

///Overall overview

Orders.IsFirstOrderOfWeek = argwhichever() by [Orders.Sku , monday(Orders.Day)]
keep where Orders.RndBaseDemand > 0 or Orders.IsFirstOrderOfWeek

iteration = 1
itText = padLeft(text(iteration),"0",2)
checkDashboard = 51914
hits.summary: This snippet: 1. Adjusts `RndBaseDemand` for extreme demand patterns. 2. Displays a test table with SKU demand metrics. 3. Calculates weekly base demand from bucket data. 4. Filters orders with positive demand or first-of-week.

hits.text:
SeasonMonthnum : number

read "/Manual/TopItemsParameters.xlsx" unsafe as TopItemsParameters with
  CategoryDisplayRank : number
  Category : text
  ItemDisplayRank : number
  Ref : text
  Sku : text
  BuyPrice : number
  MarginRatio : number
  BucketSize : number
  BaseDemand : number
  IsSeasonal : boolean
  SeasonalAgressiveness : number
  SeasonMonthnum : number

read "/Manual/Suppliers_Dashboard.ion" as Supplier with
  Supplier : text
  LeadTime : number
  "Order Frequency" as ReorderCycle : number

read form with
  startDate : date
  endDate : date
  testFocusItem : text

///~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
///---------------------------------------------CATALOG---------------------------------------------
///~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

today = date(2023,11,21)/// [Swann 21/11/2023] just created some articles with no sales. So I added a filter on the actionrwd part.
show label "Catalog" a1h1

Catalog.Sku = concat(Catalog.ItemCode,"-",Catalog.ItemLoc)
hits.summary: Creates a catalog by merging item codes and locations into SKUs, reading supplier data, item parameters, and date range inputs.

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor = 2

Orders.IsExtremeBimodalDemand = Catalog.IsBimodalDemand and
                                Orders.DayIndex mod ((1 /. Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFrequence) * Catalog.ReorderCycle / 2) == 0
                                //Division per 2 to fake twice the frequence in the orders generation while we want to display only the frequence in the distribution itself

Orders.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor = if Orders.IsExtremeBimodalDemand then Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor else 1

///Extreme right skewed demand
Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFrequence = if Catalog.IsRightSkewedDemand then 0.1 else 0
Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor = 5

Orders.IsExtremeRightSkewedDemand = Catalog.IsRightSkewedDemand and
                                    Orders.DayIndex mod ((1 /. Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFrequence) * 7) == 0

Orders.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor = if Orders.IsExtremeRightSkewedDemand then
                                              Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor * (0.2 + 0.8 * random.uniform(Orders.0,Orders.1))
    else
                                              1

///Final Rnd Base demand
hits.summary: This snippet configures extreme demand patterns for items, applying factors to orders when specific conditions (like bimodal or right-skewed demand) are met.

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
Orders.RndBucketDemand = quantile(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment, UserDefinedTimeOrders.RndQuantile)
Orders.RndDemand = Orders.RndBucketDemand * Orders.OrderWeightInBucket

///Final daily order demand
Orders.RawRndBaseDemand = max(round(Orders.RndDemand), 0)

Orders.SumRndBaseDemandByBucket = sum(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand) by [Catalog.Sku , Orders.BucketNew]

Orders.IsPickRandomBucket = argfirst() by [Catalog.Sku, Orders.BucketNew] scan Orders.RndDemand
where Orders.IsPickRandomBucket
  Orders.RawRndBaseDemand = max(Orders.RndBucketDemand - Orders.SumRndBaseDemandByBucket, 0)

///Safeguards
Catalog.SalesRanvar = ranvar(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand) by Catalog.Sku at Catalog.Sku
Catalog.SalesRanvarMean = mean(Catalog.SalesRanvar)
Catalog.SalesRanvarStd = sqrt(variance(Catalog.SalesRanvar))

Catalog.SalesRanvarQ99 = quantile(Catalog.SalesRanvar, 0.99)

Orders.RawRndBaseDemand = min(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand, min(Catalog.SalesRanvarMean + 4 * Catalog.SalesRanvarStd, Catalog.SalesRanvarQ99))

///Extreme bimodal demand
Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFrequence = if Catalog.IsBimodalDemand then 0.2 else 0
Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor = 2
hits.summary: Calculates randomized order demand per SKU/bucket, applies safeguards, and adjusts for extreme bimodal demand patterns. Writes to `Orders.RawRndBaseDemand` and `Catalog`.

  Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFrequence

  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BaseDemand

show scatter same("\{Catalog.ItemCode} - Bucket Base Demand + Growth") by slice a17b18 slices:slice with
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BaseDemandPlusGrowth

show scatter same("\{Catalog.ItemCode} - Bucket Base (Base Demand + Growth + Seasonality)") by slice f14h16 slices:slice with
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketBase

show scatter same("\{Catalog.ItemCode} - Bucket Random quantile") by slice f18h19 slices:slice with
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.RndQuantile

show linechart same("\{Catalog.ItemCode} - Orders generation") by slice c9h12 slices:slice with
  sum(Orders.RndBucketDemand) as "RndBucketDemand"
  sum(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand) as "RawRndBaseDemand"
  sum(Orders.RndBaseDemand) as "RndBaseDemand" {color: #FF7300}
  if any(Orders.IsExtremeBimodalDemand) then 1 else 0 as "Bimodal extreme point" {backgroundColor: "red" ; seriesType: background ; color: #F3E4E4}

hits.text:
Seasons.IsSeasonal = same(TopItemsParameters.IsSeasonal) by TopItemsParameters.Sku at Catalog.Sku
Seasons.SeasonalAgressiveness = same(TopItemsParameters.SeasonalAgressiveness) by TopItemsParameters.Sku at Catalog.Sku
Seasons.SeasonMonthnum = same(TopItemsParameters.SeasonMonthnum) by TopItemsParameters.Sku at Catalog.Sku

Seasons.SelectedMonthnum = if Seasons.IsSeasonal then Seasons.SeasonMonthnum else
      if Seasons.IsSeasonalSubCategory then Seasons.SubCategorySeasonMonthnum else 1

///Seasons models
pi = 3.14
maxWeekNum = max(Seasons.WeekNum)
maxSeasonalImpact = 2.5
// minSeasonalImpact = 0.5

Seasons.SeasonIndex = ((Seasons.SelectedMonthnum * 4 + 4) - Seasons.WeekNum) * (2 * pi) / maxWeekNum + pi / 2

//Low impact (Bhaskara approximation for sinus function)
sinusParam1 = 16
sinusParam2 = 4
Seasons.ClassicSinus = sinusParam1 * Seasons.SeasonIndex * (pi - Seasons.SeasonIndex) / (5 * pi^2 - sinusParam2 * Seasons.SeasonIndex * (pi - Seasons.SeasonIndex))
Seasons.MinClassicSinus = abs(min(Seasons.ClassicSinus) by Catalog.Sku)
Seasons.PositiveClassicSinus = Seasons.ClassicSinus + Seasons.MinClassicSinus
hits.summary: This snippet assigns seasonal attributes to products (from *TopItemsParameters*) and calculates a seasonal impact curve using a sine approximation. Writes to *Seasons* table.

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor = 2

Orders.IsExtremeBimodalDemand = Catalog.IsBimodalDemand and
                                Orders.DayIndex mod ((1 /. Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFrequence) * Catalog.ReorderCycle / 2) == 0
                                //Division per 2 to fake twice the frequence in the orders generation while we want to display only the frequence in the distribution itself

Orders.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor = if Orders.IsExtremeBimodalDemand then Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor else 1

///Extreme right skewed demand
Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFrequence = if Catalog.IsRightSkewedDemand then 0.1 else 0
Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor = 5

Orders.IsExtremeRightSkewedDemand = Catalog.IsRightSkewedDemand and
                                    Orders.DayIndex mod ((1 /. Catalog.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFrequence) * 7) == 0

Orders.ExtremeRightSkewedDemandFactor = if Orders.IsExtremeRightSkewedDemand then

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
Seasons.IsSeasonal = same(TopItemsParameters.IsSeasonal) by TopItemsParameters.Sku at Catalog.Sku
Seasons.SeasonalAgressiveness = same(TopItemsParameters.SeasonalAgressiveness) by TopItemsParameters.Sku at Catalog.Sku
Seasons.SeasonMonthnum = same(TopItemsParameters.SeasonMonthnum) by TopItemsParameters.Sku at Catalog.Sku

Seasons.SelectedMonthnum = if Seasons.IsSeasonal then Seasons.SeasonMonthnum else
      if Seasons.IsSeasonalSubCategory then Seasons.SubCategorySeasonMonthnum else 1

///Seasons models
pi = 3.14
maxWeekNum = max(Seasons.WeekNum)
maxSeasonalImpact = 2.5
// minSeasonalImpact = 0.5

Seasons.SeasonIndex = ((Seasons.SelectedMonthnum * 4 + 4) - Seasons.WeekNum) * (2 * pi) / maxWeekNum + pi / 2

//Low impact (Bhaskara approximation for sinus function)
sinusParam1 = 16
sinusParam2 = 4
Seasons.ClassicSinus = sinusParam1 * Seasons.SeasonIndex * (pi - Seasons.SeasonIndex) / (5 * pi^2 - sinusParam2 * Seasons.SeasonIndex * (pi - Seasons.SeasonIndex))
Seasons.MinClassicSinus = abs(min(Seasons.ClassicSinus) by Catalog.Sku)
Seasons.PositiveClassicSinus = Seasons.ClassicSinus + Seasons.MinClassicSinus
hits.summary: This snippet assigns seasonal attributes to products (from *TopItemsParameters*) and calculates a seasonal impact curve using a sine approximation. Writes to *Seasons* table.

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
formendDate = if endDate == date(2001,01,01) then today() else endDate

keep where Orders.Day >= formstartDate and Orders.Day <= (formendDate + 365)

Orders.OrderId = concat("O-",padLeft(text(Orders.Day - date(2001,01,01)),"0",6))

Orders.BucketNew = ceiling(Orders.DayIndex / Catalog.PeriodGroupSize)

Orders.IsPast = Orders.Day <= formendDate
UserDefinedTimeOrders.IsPast = any(Orders.IsPast)

///Seasonality--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

///Weekly catalog
Catalog.CountWeeks = 54
table Seasons = extend.range(Catalog.CountWeeks)
Seasons.WeekNum = Seasons.N

Seasons.SubCategory = Catalog.SubCategory

///Seasons parameters
Seasons.IsSeasonalSubCategory = same(TopSubCategories.IsSeasonal) by TopSubCategories.SubCategory at Catalog.SubCategory
Seasons.SubCategorySeasonalAgressiveness = same(TopSubCategories.SeasonalAgressiveness) by TopSubCategories.SubCategory at Catalog.SubCategory
Seasons.SubCategorySeasonMonthnum = same(TopSubCategories.SeasonMonthnum) by TopSubCategories.SubCategory at Catalog.SubCategory

Seasons.IsSeasonal = same(TopItemsParameters.IsSeasonal) by TopItemsParameters.Sku at Catalog.Sku
hits.summary: Adjusts date range, filters orders, formats IDs, and sets time-based flags (past/bucket). Defines weekly catalog seasons with seasonal subcategory attributes. (24 words)

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
Seasons.IsSeasonal = same(TopItemsParameters.IsSeasonal) by TopItemsParameters.Sku at Catalog.Sku
Seasons.SeasonalAgressiveness = same(TopItemsParameters.SeasonalAgressiveness) by TopItemsParameters.Sku at Catalog.Sku
Seasons.SeasonMonthnum = same(TopItemsParameters.SeasonMonthnum) by TopItemsParameters.Sku at Catalog.Sku

Seasons.SelectedMonthnum = if Seasons.IsSeasonal then Seasons.SeasonMonthnum else
      if Seasons.IsSeasonalSubCategory then Seasons.SubCategorySeasonMonthnum else 1

///Seasons models
pi = 3.14
maxWeekNum = max(Seasons.WeekNum)
maxSeasonalImpact = 2.5
// minSeasonalImpact = 0.5

Seasons.SeasonIndex = ((Seasons.SelectedMonthnum * 4 + 4) - Seasons.WeekNum) * (2 * pi) / maxWeekNum + pi / 2

//Low impact (Bhaskara approximation for sinus function)
sinusParam1 = 16
sinusParam2 = 4
Seasons.ClassicSinus = sinusParam1 * Seasons.SeasonIndex * (pi - Seasons.SeasonIndex) / (5 * pi^2 - sinusParam2 * Seasons.SeasonIndex * (pi - Seasons.SeasonIndex))
Seasons.MinClassicSinus = abs(min(Seasons.ClassicSinus) by Catalog.Sku)
Seasons.PositiveClassicSinus = Seasons.ClassicSinus + Seasons.MinClassicSinus
hits.summary: This snippet assigns seasonal attributes to products (from *TopItemsParameters*) and calculates a seasonal impact curve using a sine approximation. Writes to *Seasons* table.

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.summary: Calculates weekly sales revenue by item rank (4-9) from **Orders** and **Catalog**, then charts order quantities for ranks 1-4.

hits.file_path: /1. utilities/1. populating dataset/01 - Catalog and Orders History Creation
hits.text:
Orders.RndBucketDemand = quantile(UserDefinedTimeOrders.DemandSegment, UserDefinedTimeOrders.RndQuantile)
Orders.RndDemand = Orders.RndBucketDemand * Orders.OrderWeightInBucket

///Final daily order demand
Orders.RawRndBaseDemand = max(round(Orders.RndDemand), 0)

Orders.SumRndBaseDemandByBucket = sum(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand) by [Catalog.Sku , Orders.BucketNew]

Orders.IsPickRandomBucket = argfirst() by [Catalog.Sku, Orders.BucketNew] scan Orders.RndDemand
where Orders.IsPickRandomBucket
  Orders.RawRndBaseDemand = max(Orders.RndBucketDemand - Orders.SumRndBaseDemandByBucket, 0)

///Safeguards
Catalog.SalesRanvar = ranvar(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand) by Catalog.Sku at Catalog.Sku
Catalog.SalesRanvarMean = mean(Catalog.SalesRanvar)
Catalog.SalesRanvarStd = sqrt(variance(Catalog.SalesRanvar))

Catalog.SalesRanvarQ99 = quantile(Catalog.SalesRanvar, 0.99)

Orders.RawRndBaseDemand = min(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand, min(Catalog.SalesRanvarMean + 4 * Catalog.SalesRanvarStd, Catalog.SalesRanvarQ99))

///Extreme bimodal demand
Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFrequence = if Catalog.IsBimodalDemand then 0.2 else 0
Catalog.ExtremeBimodalDemandFactor = 2
hits.summary: Calculates randomized order demand per SKU/bucket, applies safeguards, and adjusts for extreme bimodal demand patterns. Writes to `Orders.RawRndBaseDemand` and `Catalog`.

show scatter same("\{Catalog.ItemCode} - Bucket Base Demand") by slice a15b16 slices:slice with

  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BaseDemandPlusGrowth

show scatter same("\{Catalog.ItemCode} - Bucket Base (Base Demand + Growth + Seasonality)") by slice f14h16 slices:slice with
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketIndex
  UserDefinedTimeOrders.BucketBase

show scatter same("\{Catalog.ItemCode} - Bucket Random quantile") by slice f18h19 slices:slice with

  UserDefinedTimeOrders.RndQuantile

show linechart same("\{Catalog.ItemCode} - Orders generation") by slice c9h12 slices:slice with
  sum(Orders.RndBucketDemand) as "RndBucketDemand"
  sum(Orders.RawRndBaseDemand) as "RawRndBaseDemand"
  sum(Orders.RndBaseDemand) as "RndBaseDemand" {color: #FF7300}
  if any(Orders.IsExtremeBimodalDemand) then 1 else 0 as "Bimodal extreme point" {backgroundColor: "red" ; seriesType: background ; color: #F3E4E4}
